2605.12382.title: Pretraining Exposure Explains Popularity Judgments in Large Language Models
2605.12382.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit systematic preferences for well-known entities, a phenomenon often attributed to popularity bias. However, the extent to which these preferences reflect real-world popularity versus statistical exposure during pretraining remains unclear, largely due to the inaccessibility of most training corpora. We provide the first direct, large-scale analysis of popularity bias grounded in fully observable pretraining data. Leveraging the open OLMo models and their complete pretraining corpus, Dolma, we compute precise entity-level exposure statistics across 7.4 trillion tokens. We analyze 2,000 entities spanning five types (Person, Location, Organization, Art, Product) and compare pretraining exposure against Wikipedia pageviews and two elicited LLM popularity signals: direct scalar estimation and pairwise comparison. Our results show that pretraining exposure strongly correlates with Wikipedia popularity, validating exposure as a meaningful proxy for real-world salience during the training period. More importantly, we find that LLM popularity judgments align more closely with exposure than with Wikipedia, especially when elicited via pairwise comparisons. This alignment is strongest for larger models and persists in the long tail, where Wikipedia popularity becomes unreliable. Overall, our findings demonstrate that popularity priors in LLMs are primarily shaped by pretraining statistics rather than external popularity signals, offering concrete evidence that data exposure plays a central role in driving popularity bias.
2605.12382.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）對於知名實體表現出系統性的偏好，這一現象通常歸因於流行偏見。然而，這些偏好在多大程度上反映了現實世界的流行程度與預訓練期間的統計曝光之間的關係仍然不清楚，主要是由於大多數訓練語料庫的不可獲得性。我們提供了首個基於完全可觀察的預訓練數據的直接大規模流行偏見分析。利用開放的OLMo模型及其完整的預訓練語料庫Dolma，我們計算了7.4萬億個標記的精確實體級曝光統計。我們分析了涵蓋五種類型（人、地點、組織、藝術、產品）的2,000個實體，並將預訓練曝光與維基百科頁面瀏覽量及兩個引導的LLM流行信號進行比較：直接標量估計和成對比較。我們的結果顯示，預訓練曝光與維基百科的流行程度強烈相關，驗證了曝光在訓練期間作為現實世界顯著性的有意義代理的作用。更重要的是，我們發現LLM的流行判斷與曝光的對應關係比與維基百科更為緊密，特別是在通過成對比較引導時。這種對應關係在較大的模型中最為強烈，並在長尾中持續存在，當維基百科的流行性變得不可靠時。總體而言，我們的研究結果表明，LLMs中的流行先驗主要受到預訓練統計的影響，而非外部流行信號，提供了具體證據表明數據曝光在驅動流行偏見中扮演了核心角色。
2605.12382.author: Jamshid Mozafari et.al.
2605.12382.authors: Jamshid Mozafari, Bhawna Piryani, Adam Jatowt
